step_by_step_thinking:
Acrocephaly is another name for oxycephaly, which describes a cranial deformity characterized by a prominent forehead. Therefore, acrocephaly falls under the broader category D: All of the above since "oxycephaly" is one of the listed options.
\boxed{D} Based on the definitions and context given in the medical evidence, particularly focusing on descriptions of different types of craniofacial deformities and classifications used in obstetrics and pediatrics, acrocephaly fits within the broader category defined by option D: All of the above. Since oxycephaly is explicitly mentioned as one possible description alongside others like turticephaly and dome-shaped skull, acrocephaly aligns with being included in this wider definition.
